title: Fix stored XSS in dashboard.html via innerHTML
description: attached_molecule: sp-wisp-ewbw attached_formula: mol-polecat-work attached_at: 2026-04-18T21:00:11Z dispatched_by: unknown The dashboard assembles table rows and cards by interpolating scraped third-party strings into template literals assigned to .innerHTML (lines 636-638, 670-675, 1003-1006, 1010-1014, 1091-1095, 1313-1317, 1471-1480 of app/templates/dashboard.html). title: Fix SSRF in practiscore_scraper.py - validate URLs before fetch
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-0yu3
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T21:01:24Z
dispatched_by: unknown

In app/services/practiscore_scraper.py (lines 63–83, 217–224, 291–306), absolute URLs scraped from HTML are used verbatim as the next fetch target with no scheme or host validation. An attacker who controls a PractiScore match page can supply hrefs pointing at AWS IMDS (169.254.169.254), localhost, or internal Docker services (redis, postgres).

Fix: Before any fetch in _fetch_with_fallback and wherever href is resolved, validate that:
1. The URL scheme is http or https only
2. The netloc matches an allowlist: only practiscore.com and www.practiscore.com are permitted
3. Reject any URL that fails these checks with a log warning rather than fetching it

Use urllib.parse.urlparse() for parsing. Reject relative paths that don't start with an allowlisted host.

title: Fix docker-compose: remove host port exposure for postgres/redis, add Redis auth
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-6cyy
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-19T06:54:13Z
dispatched_by: unknown

In mayor/rig/docker-compose.yml (and if present refinery/rig/docker-compose.yml), postgres publishes 5432:5432 and redis publishes 6379:6379 to all host interfaces (0.0.0.0). Default POSTGRES_PASSWORD is 'postgres' and Redis has no requirepass. This exposes both services to anyone on the network.

Fix:
1. Remove the 'ports:' sections entirely for postgres and redis services - in-compose services communicate on the Docker internal network without host publishing. If debugging access is needed locally, bind to 127.0.0.1:5432:5432 (do not remove entirely if the dev workflow requires it, but at minimum bind to localhost).
2. Add Redis password auth: add 'command: ["redis-server", "--requirepass", "${REDIS_PASSWORD}"]' to the redis service, and add REDIS_PASSWORD to the environment section.
3. Update .env.example to include REDIS_PASSWORD=<set-strong-password> with a placeholder value (not a real password).

title: Add Subresource Integrity hashes to CDN scripts in base.html
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-1t2s
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T20:57:04Z
dispatched_by: unknown

app/templates/base.html loads htmx, chart.js, and chartjs-adapter-date-fns from CDNs (unpkg.com, cdn.jsdelivr.net) without integrity= or crossorigin= attributes (lines 9–13). A CDN compromise or DNS hijack could serve malicious JS to all dashboard users.

Fix: For each external script tag, compute the SRI hash and add integrity and crossorigin attributes. The three scripts are:
- https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@1.9.12/dist/htmx.min.js
- https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.4.3/dist/chart.umd.min.js (or similar path)
- https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chartjs-adapter-date-fns@3.0.0/dist/chartjs-adapter-date-fns.bundle.min.js (or similar path)

Steps:
1. Download each file with curl to /tmp
2. Compute hash: openssl dgst -sha384 -binary file.js | openssl base64 -A
3. Add to the script tag: integrity='sha384-<hash>' crossorigin='anonymous'

Do the same for any CSS link tags loaded from CDNs in base.html.
close_reason: Implemented SRI integrity and crossorigin attributes for all three CDN scripts in base.html

title: Fix rate limiter proxy IP bypass and add security headers middleware
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-61pl
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T20:56:13Z
dispatched_by: unknown

Two medium-severity issues:

1. RATE LIMITER (app/limiter.py): Uses get_remote_address which returns the TCP peer IP. Behind any reverse proxy this is always the proxy IP, making rate limits effectively global. Fix: Replace get_remote_address with a custom key_func that reads X-Real-IP or the rightmost untrusted element of X-Forwarded-For. Example:
   from starlette.requests import Request
   def get_client_ip(request: Request) -> str:
       forwarded_for = request.headers.get('X-Forwarded-For')
       if forwarded_for:
           return forwarded_for.split(',')[0].strip()
       return request.client.host if request.client else '127.0.0.1'
   limiter = Limiter(key_func=get_client_ip)

2. SECURITY HEADERS (app/main.py): No security headers are set. Fix: Add a middleware that sets these response headers on every request:
   - Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://unpkg.com https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'
   - X-Frame-Options: DENY
   - X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
   - Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
   - Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()
   Also add Starlette's TrustedHostMiddleware for expected hostnames.
   Implement as a simple @app.middleware('http') async function in main.py.

title: Add asyncio.Semaphore to cap concurrent Playwright browser instances
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-4q2o
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T20:57:52Z
dispatched_by: unknown

In app/services/uspsa_scraper.py, _fetch_with_playwright launches a fresh Playwright/Chromium browser per request with no cap on total concurrent instances. Combined with the rate limiter bypass, this allows a cheap CPU/memory DoS (tens of MB RAM + non-trivial CPU per browser, up to 30s per Cloudflare challenge).

Fix:
1. Create a module-level asyncio.Semaphore(3) in uspsa_scraper.py (or task_manager.py if more appropriate) limiting concurrent Playwright invocations to 3.
2. Wrap the Playwright browser launch block with 'async with _playwright_semaphore:' so at most 3 browsers run simultaneously.
3. If possible, reuse a single Playwright browser instance across requests (browser singleton with async lock) instead of launching one per job - this dramatically reduces overhead.
4. Add a circuit breaker: track consecutive Cloudflare-challenge timeouts; after 3 consecutive failures, skip Playwright and return a 503-like error for a 60-second cooldown window.

title: Add uv lockfile and swap psycopg2-binary for psycopg2
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-wbm2
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T21:03:19Z
dispatched_by: unknown

pyproject.toml uses >= lower bounds only with no lockfile, so CI/Docker builds can resolve different transitive versions over time. Also, psycopg2-binary ships a vendored libpq that defeats system-level patching of OpenSSL.

Fix:
1. Run 'uv lock' to generate uv.lock from the existing pyproject.toml dependencies. Commit uv.lock to the repo.
2. Update Dockerfile to install with 'uv sync --frozen' (or 'pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt' if exporting) so the lockfile is enforced in the container build.
3. In pyproject.toml, swap 'psycopg2-binary' for 'psycopg2' (source build). The Dockerfile already has 'libpq-dev' installed (or add it if missing) so the source build will succeed.
4. Update the Dockerfile build steps if needed to ensure uv is available (e.g. 'pip install uv' or use the uv Docker image as base).
5. Verify the app still starts and tests pass after the swap.

title: Sanitize exception messages returned via job status API
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-4tls
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-18T21:03:59Z
dispatched_by: unknown

In app/services/task_manager.py, job_status[job_id]['error'] = str(exc) stores raw exception strings including DB errors with DETAIL lines (table names, constraint names, key values), internal URLs, and curl_cffi stack traces. These are returned verbatim by app/routes/members.py get_member_status via resp['error'] = job.get('error', 'Unknown error').

Fix:
1. In task_manager.py, store two fields per job: 'error_public' (safe user-facing message) and 'error_internal' (str(exc) for server-side logging only).
2. Map known exception types to stable public messages:
   - DB integrity errors → 'Data processing error, please retry'
   - Network/scraping errors → 'Scraping failed, please retry'
   - Timeout errors → 'Request timed out, please retry'
   - Default fallback → 'An unexpected error occurred'
3. Log the full str(exc) at ERROR level with the job_id for server-side debugging.
4. In get_member_status route, return only error_public, never error_internal.
5. While in task_manager.py, also add cleanup for completed/errored job entries: after a job reaches terminal state (done/error), schedule removal after 1 hour to prevent unbounded dict growth (use a simple timestamp check on next access or a background cleanup task).

Build all API routes in app/routes/.

    - Validates member number (alphanumeric, 5-10 chars)

app/routes/health.py:

Add rate limiting: max 10 requests/minute per IP using slowapi.
Input validation with pydantic.
Structured error responses: { error: str, detail: str, code: int }.

title: Caching layer, background tasks, and rate limiting
description:
attached_molecule: sp-wisp-wkpgi
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T17:40:36Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Add production-ready caching and background task support.

1. In-memory cache (app/services/cache.py):
   - TTLCache class: dict-based with expiry timestamps
   - get(key), set(key, value, ttl=86400), delete(key), clear()
   - Keyed by member_number

2. Background scraping (app/services/task_manager.py):
   - job_status dict: { job_id: {status, member_number, started_at, completed_at, error} }
   - async scrape_and_store(member_number, db_session) coroutine
   - Updates DB and cache on completion

3. Rate limiting:
   - Use slowapi (Starlette-compatible)
   - 10 requests/min per IP on /api/analyze
   - 60 requests/min per IP on /api/member GET endpoints

4. Cache invalidation:
   - Force refresh via ?refresh=true on dashboard endpoint
   - Auto-expire at CACHE_TTL (env var, default 86400s)

Wire everything into main.py.

title: mol-polecat-work
description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (pinned molecule + issue)
2. Work through molecule steps using `bd ready` / `bd close <step>`
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the template. Your molecule already has step
beads created from it. Use `bd ready` to find them - do NOT read this file directly.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Handle rebase conflicts (Refinery spawns fresh polecats for that)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (pinned molecule + issue)
2. Work through molecule steps using `bd ready` / `bd close <step>`
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the template. Your molecule already has step
beads created from it. Use `bd ready` to find them - do NOT read this file directly.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Handle rebase conflicts (Refinery spawns fresh polecats for that)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
